name: ExchangeRate-API / exchangerate.host
what_it_does: Provides real-time and historical foreign exchange rates, currency conversion, and supported currency lists. Covers 160+ fiat currencies with rates sourced from central banks and financial data providers. Some providers also support cryptocurrency rates.
use_cases: Converting prices between currencies in e-commerce or invoicing agents
Displaying localized pricing with real-time FX rates in multi-currency apps
Calculating historical exchange rates for financial reporting or audit agents
Monitoring FX rate changes and triggering alerts when thresholds are crossed
Normalizing financial data across currencies for analytics pipelines
not_for: Live trading or arbitrage (rates are indicative, not executable)
Tick-level FX data for algorithmic trading strategies
best_when: An agent needs straightforward currency conversion or FX rate lookup for display, reporting, or normalization purposes and does not need bank-grade precision or executable rates.
avoid_when: You need real-time interbank rates with sub-second updates, executable FX quotes, or rates for exotic/illiquid currency pairs.

auth.notes: API key passed as part of the URL path (ExchangeRate-API style: /v6/{api_key}/latest/{base}) or as a query parameter depending on provider variant. No OAuth or scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1,500 requests/month on free plan (ExchangeRate-API); exchangerate.host offers a free tier with no key required for basic endpoints
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro $9.99/mo (100K requests, historical data), Business $19.99/mo (1M requests, forex data), Volume plans available
pricing.notes: ExchangeRate-API and exchangerate.host are distinct services with similar names. Free tier sufficient for light agent usage. Historical data and some currencies require paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits vary by plan; free tier has monthly request caps. No documented per-second limits but burst behavior may be throttled.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors returned as JSON with result: 'error' and error-type field. Error types are string enums like 'invalid-key', 'inactive-account', 'quota-reached'. Parseable but error type strings are not always self-explanatory for agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only and fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: ExchangeRate-API and exchangerate.host are two different services with near-identical names - agents must be configured for the correct provider endpoint
Rates are updated at different frequencies per plan (hourly on free, every 60 seconds on paid) - agents needing fresh rates must check update time from response
API key embedded in URL path (not header) makes key rotation harder and exposes it in server access logs
Free plans do not include historical rates - agents querying past dates will get 403 errors without clear messaging about plan requirements
Base currency on free plans may be restricted to USD or EUR - cross-currency pairs require paid plan or two-step conversion

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key in URL path is a weak pattern - key visible in server logs, browser history, and referrer headers. No scopes or IP restrictions. For agents, key should be kept server-side and rotated periodically. No documented security advisory process.
